checks.a_analysis: The output follows the structure and content of the source document closely. The metadata title 'Logo Imagery Screenshot Imagery Fca1' is an AI-generated title, which is an acceptable change per the instructions. There are no invented paragraphs, section headings, or body text not present in the source.

The URL 'https://myncid.nc.gov/' appears in the source and is correctly reproduced in several sections of the output. No new or incorrect URLs, emails, or phone numbers have been added to the document.

All eight sections ('How to register', 'How to unlock your account', 'How to retrieve your username', 'How to reset your password', 'How to change your password', 'How to update your profile', 'How to enroll Open Subscription Applications', and 'How to delete your account') across the three-page source document are present in the output.
The source document does not contain any tables. This check is not applicable.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: While there are several omissions of critical short strings (URLs and button labels) in the latter half of the document, these omissions do not exceed the 25% threshold for any individual section. The sections remain mostly intact in terms of instructional steps and general text volume.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: There are several instances where text from the source is omitted, specifically URLs and button labels that were part of the instructions. In the sections 'How to reset your password', 'How to change your password', 'How to update your profile', 'How to enroll...', and 'How to delete...', the URL 'https://myncid.nc.gov/' is missing after the string 'Go to '. Additionally, button labels such as 'Submit', 'Reset', and 'SAVE' are missing in the respective 'Click' steps within the 'How to reset your password', 'How to change your password', and 'How to update your profile' sections. These omissions change the information provided, as the instructions are left incomplete and lose their target destination or action label.
checks.b_evidence_source: 1. Go to https://myncid.nc.gov/ ; 6. Enter the OTP sent to you and click: Submit ; 8. Click on: Reset ; 5. Click on: SAVE ; 4. Edit the values that you want to update, click on: SAVE

checks.a_analysis: The output was scanned for duplicated text blocks and repeated sections. While certain phrases such as 'Trouble Signing On?' and 'Select the account type (Individual or business)' appear multiple times, this reflects the intentional repetition in the source document across different instructional modules. No accidental duplications of text or sections were found.

The output maintains a consistent heading hierarchy. The document title 'MyNCID User Guide' is correctly identified as an H1, and the various 'How to...' instructional sections are consistently labeled as H2. There is no evidence of skipped heading levels (e.g., H1 to H3).
The source document contains instructional columns but no semantic tables (rows/columns/headers). Consequently, no tables were expected or identified in the output.

The reading order in the JSON follows the logical flow of the document. It processes page 1 from left to right columns, followed by page 2, and then page 3. While images are sometimes grouped at the end of their respective sections rather than immediately following the specific step they illustrate, the pipeline has successfully integrated the text within those images (e.g., button labels like 'SAVE' or 'CONFIRM') directly into the text flow, preserving the instructional logic.

The source document uses numbered (ordered) lists for every section. The output fails to preserve the list type, marking all lists as 'unordered'. Additionally, many of the numbered lists are split into multiple separate list objects in the JSON due to the interference of image layout (e.g., 'How to register' is split into two lists at index 3 and 4). Furthermore, the actual numbers (1., 2., etc.) have been stripped from the text content.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 1, 'How to register' section contains a single numbered list from steps 1 to 6.
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections 'How to register', 'How to unlock your account', element indices 3, 4, 7, 8, 9.

checks.a_analysis: The source document contains several instances of the URL 'https://myncid.nc.gov/'. In the output, these are rendered as plain text strings within the list items. No semantic link elements or clickable URL objects were generated, making the links 'missing' from a functional/structural standpoint.

checks.a_analysis: The visual layout of the document is significantly compromised by the extraction of instructional images (buttons and links) from their corresponding text flow. In the source PDF, images for buttons like 'SAVE', 'CONFIRM', 'Next', and 'Submit' are placed immediately following the instructional text (e.g., 'Click [button image]'). In the HTML output, the text instructions are grouped separately from these images. The button images are rendered in detached blocks after several sections of text, leaving the instructions in the main body incomplete and nonsensical (e.g., sentences ending with 'Click' or 'click:' followed by a blank space). This rearrangement breaks the visual flow and the original intent of the instructional guide.
checks.b_evidence_source: PDF Page 1, 'How to register' step 4: 'Click [SAVE button image]'; step 5: 'click [CONFIRM button image]'. PDF Page 1, 'How to unlock your account' step 5: 'click [Next button image]'; step 7: 'click: [Submit button image]'.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML sections for 'How to register' and 'How to unlock your account' show these same sentences ending abruptly with 'Click' or 'click:', with the corresponding button images appearing much later in a separate group of image blocks.
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections 'How to register', 'How to unlock your account', and 'How to retrieve your username', page 1 of PDF.

checks.a_analysis: The source document does not contain any tables. The information is presented in columns and lists, which are faithfully represented as headings and bulleted lists in the HTML.

While most images and buttons have been captured (though poorly placed), at least one meaningful instructional image is entirely missing from the HTML output. Specifically, the screenshot of the 'delete' confirmation dialog box found on Page 3, which is critical for the 'How to delete your account' instructions, is not present in the rendered HTML screenshot.
checks.b_evidence_source: PDF Page 3, 'How to delete your account' step 4 shows a screenshot of a dialog box containing the text 'Type "delete" to confirm', a text input field with the word 'delete' entered, and 'CANCEL' and 'DELETE' buttons.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML output for the 'How to delete your account' section and the subsequent collection of image assets at the end of the document do not include this confirmation dialog screenshot.
checks.b_evidence_location: Section 'How to delete your account', PDF page 3.

checks.a_analysis: There is no evidence of unintended content duplication in the body of the document. Repeated elements, such as the 'Trouble Signing On?' button or the link blocks, are present multiple times in the source PDF across different instructional sections, and their recurrence in the HTML faithfully represents those multiple instances.
